title: Broken toe - NHS
text:
not strap up your toe if it's pointing out at an odd angle or you have hurt your big toe – get medical advice
do not put ice directly on your skin
do not walk or stand for long periods
do not wear tight, pointy shoes
do not play any sports like football, rugby or hockey for 6 weeks or until the pain eases
do not try to treat your child's toe – take them to an urgent treatment centre or A&E
A pharmacist can help with a broken toe You can ask a pharmacist about:
the best painkiller to take what you need to strap up your toe if you need to see a GP 
Find a pharmacy 
Non-urgent advice: See a GP if:
pain and swelling has not started to ease 2 to 3 days after you injured your toe it still hurts to walk 6 weeks after injuring your toe you have diabetes and have injured your toe – foot problems can be more serious if you have diabetes They may send you for an X-ray to see if you need any further treatment.
Page last reviewed: 06 May 2022
Next review due: 06 May 2025
